reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Spanish, preserving frontmatter, code blocks, MDX components, etc. Must translate only reader-facing text: frontmatter values: title, subTitle, maybe cover_alt, cover_credit (none present). Keep language field? There's no language field currently; we need to add? The instruction: If a language field exists, set it exactly to "Spanish". There's none, so we don't add. Keep other metadata unchanged. We need to translate the prose, headings, blockquotes, etc. Keep code unchanged. For relative URLs, need to prefix with ../ . The image URLs: "./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg", "./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp". They are relative; need to prefix with ../ . So change to "../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg"? Actually original path is "./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg". According to rule: ALL relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with a `../`. So we need to change "./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg" to "../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg". Similarly "./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp". Also cover images in frontmatter: cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp etc. Those are relative paths; need to prefix with ../ . So change to "../hero-wide.webp", etc. Also any other relative URLs: the link to Internet-Draft: https://datatracker.ietf.org/... is absolute, keep unchanged. Now translate headings: Title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" => "Es hora de las cadenas de conexión llm://". SubTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" => "Simplifica la configuración de modelo y proveedor con URLs `llm://`". Keep code formatting. Now translate blockquote text: "**Update:** This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](...).". Translate "Update:" to "Actualización:" and keep link text maybe "Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme" translate to "borrador de Internet para el esquema URI `llm://`". Keep link. Then the prose paragraphs. Need to preserve paragraph breaks. Translate "Remember the bad old days when connecting to a database meant juggling a miscellaneous grab-bag of environment variables?" => "¿Recuerdas los malos tiempos en los que conectar a una base de datos implicaba manejar una mezcolanza de variables de entorno?" Something like that. Continue. Make sure to keep code blocks unchanged. Translate "It was a tower of delicate config. `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`... or wait, was it `DB_USERNAME`? Is it `DB_PASS` or `DB_PWD`? Do I need the `PG_*` prefixes this time? And where the hell does the timeout setting go?" => "Era una torre de configuración delicada. `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`... o espera, ¿era `DB_USERNAME`? ¿Es `DB_PASS` o `DB_PWD`? ¿Necesito los prefijos `PG_*` esta vez? ¿Y dónde demonios va la configuración del timeout?" Keep tone. "It was a fragile house of cards, ready to topple your production build because you forgot to capitalize `HOST`." => "Era una frágil casa de naipes, lista para derribar tu compilación de producción porque olvidaste capitalizar `HOST`." Then "Then, someone had the brilliant idea to just use a URL¹:" => "Entonces, alguien tuvo la brillante idea de usar simplemente una URL¹:" Then code block unchanged. "One string. Everything you need. Universally parseable. Portable. Dare I say... beautiful?" => "Una sola cadena. Todo lo que necesitas. Universalmente parseable. Portable. ¿Me atrevo a decir... hermosa?" Keep tone. "So why are we treating LLMs like it's 1999?" => "Entonces, ¿por qué tratamos a los LLM como si fuera 1999?" maybe "¿Por qué tratamos a los LLM como si fuera 1999?" Keep. Heading "## The Env Var Explosion" => "## La explosión de variables de entorno". Keep. Paragraph: "Right now, my `.env` file looks like a graveyard of abandoned API keys. `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MISTRAL_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`. And don't get me started on Azure—you need an endpoint, a deployment name, an API version, and a key just to say "hello"." => translate. "It’s not just ugly; it’s friction. Every time I want to swap a model or test a new provider, I'm rewriting initialization code, hunting down documentation for specific parameter names, and adding three more lines to my environment config." => translate. "What if we just... ~~stole~~ borrowed the DB URL idea?" => translate, keep strikethrough. Heading "## Introducing LLM Connection Strings" => "## Introduciendo cadenas de conexión LLM". Paragraph: "Imagine configuring your entire model interface with a single line:" => translate. Code block unchanged. Then "---\n
\n\n### Anatomy of an LLM Connection String" => translate heading: "### Anatomía de una cadena de conexión LLM". Keep the
and horizontal rule. Image alt text: "the parts of a LLM connection string" => translate alt? It's inside markdown image: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg). Need to translate alt text: "las partes de una cadena de conexión LLM". Keep path changed. Paragraph: "The scheme is `llm://`. The host is the provider's API base URL. The path is the model name. And query parameters handle all the runtime options that usually clutter your code." => translate. Heading "## Need auth? Great, add it." => "## ¿Necesitas autenticación? Perfecto, añádela." Paragraph: "Just like `postgres://`, we can bake authentication right in:" => translate. Code block unchanged. Note: "*Note: Yes, putting credentials in URLs can be a security risk if you're pasting them into public logs. But modern logging services are pretty good at scrubbing these patterns, and honestly, are you treating your `.env` file much better? Verify, sanitize, and use with caution.*" => translate, keep emphasis. Heading "## Resiliency? Why the hell not." => "## ¿Resiliencia? ¿Por qué demonios no?" maybe "¿Resiliencia? ¿Por qué demonios no?" Keep. Paragraph: "Many database libraries support round-robin failover by specifying multiple hosts. Why shouldn't our AI agents have the same reliability?" => translate. Code block unchanged. Paragraph: "That `s` in `llms://` isn't a typo. It's plural. If `primary.gpt` hangs, the client automatically retries `backup.gpt`. No complex router logic required." => translate. Blockquote: "
One string with everything from your **auth** to your **endpoint** to your **hyperparameters**.
" => translate: "Una sola cadena con todo, desde tu **auth** hasta tu **endpoint** y tus **hiperparámetros**." Keep bold. Heading "## Alternative Formats" => "## Formatos alternativos". Paragraph: "I'm not married to `llm://`. The specific scheme matters less than the standard itself." => translate. Paragraph: "I could imagine a world where we use provider-specific schemes for brevity, while keeping the standard structure:" => translate. Code block unchanged. Paragraph: "Regardless of the exact syntax, the core benefits are undeniable:" => translate. List items: translate each. 1. "**Portability:** Copy & paste your entire config from a local script to a cloud worker." => "Portabilidad: Copia y pega toda tu configuración de un script local a un worker en la nube." 2. "**CLI Friendly:** Pass a single argument to your scripts. `my-agent --model \"llm://...\"` beats `my-agent --model gpt-4 --temp 0.7 --key $KEY --host ...`." => "Amigable con CLI: pasa un solo argumento a tus scripts. `my-agent --model \"llm://...\"` supera a `my-agent --model gpt-4 --temp 0.7 --key $KEY --host ...`." 3. "**Language Agnostic:** Every programming language has a robust URL parser. We get validation, parsing, and sanitization for free." => "Independiente del lenguaje: Cada lenguaje de programación tiene un analizador de URLs robusto. Obtienes validación, análisis y saneamiento sin costo." Blockquote with class ai-response: "
The database world took decades to figure this out.
Good news, in AI timelines, that's only about half a vibe-year ago.
" => translate: "El mundo de las bases de datos tardó décadas en descubrir esto.
Buena noticia, en la cronología de IA, eso fue hace apenas medio año de vibra." maybe adjust phrase: "medio año de vibra" is weird; maybe "medio año de evolución". But keep tone. Keep
and . Heading "## The Verdict" => "## El veredicto". Paragraph: "We don't need another complex configuration standard or a new YAML-based manifest file. We just need to use the one tool that's been working for the rest of the internet for the last 30 years." => translate. Paragraph: "Let's stop reinventing the wheel and start treating our LLM connections with the same respect we give our databases. Your `.env` file (and your sanity) will thank you." => translate. Image: ![a messy env var drawer](./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp) => alt translate: "un cajón desordenado de variables de entorno". Path change. Comment: {/* ¹ Yes, I know that `URI` is more correct... */} => translate comment text but keep braces. It's a comment in JSX? It's inside {/* ... */}. Should translate the comment content. Keep braces and code. So translate Spanish: "¹ Sí, sé que `URI` es más correcto que `URL`. Si eres lo suficientemente pedante como para preocuparte por esa distinción, por favor ve a tocar pasto." Keep same formatting. Now adjust frontmatter image paths: cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp => change to ../hero-wide.webp. cover_mobile: ./square-200.webp => ../square-200.webp. cover_icon: ./square-200.webp => ../square-200.webp. Also social_image: desktop-social.webp is relative? It's just filename, likely relative. Should prefix with ../? It's a relative path. According to rule, all relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with `../`. So social_image: desktop-social.webp => ../desktop-social.webp. Now produce final output. Check frontmatter order: keep same keys. Only translate title, subTitle. No language field. Keep other keys unchanged except modify paths. Let's construct final output.
